summary: Install-time hostname beacon to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On npm install, postinstall.js issues an HTTP request to a hardcoded bare IP (http://37.60.255.218/skybackground.png?display=<hostname>) carrying the installer's machine hostname (os.hostname()) as a query parameter. The destination is a bare IPv4 address served over plaintext HTTP with no TLS, no publisher attribution, and no documented purpose. Package metadata is deceptive: README advertises 'Simple Text Utils' with capitalize/reverse/wordCount APIs, but the actual code only exports a downloadImage function, the package.json author is the placeholder 'Your Name', and the package name 'micro-ui-loader' matches neither the README nor the exported API. The combination of install-time outbound beacon disclosing host identifiers to attacker infrastructure plus deceptive packaging is a recon/exfiltration supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
